answer:
\boxed{Numa Pompilius}

The question refers to the legendary Roman king **Numa Pompilius**, whose daughter **Rhea Silvia** (or **Egeria**) was said to have been buried with a still-burning lamp in her tomb. This discovery was later attributed to the papacy of **Paul III (1534–1549)**, though the tale is more mythological than historical. The story blends Roman legend with medieval/early modern interpretations of ancient relics.
